title: Support parameter names in pattern initialization
body:
I'm trying to have verilator compile an SoC project, the AHB/AXI buses make use of the following pattern to assign interfaces to crossbars/arbiters, etc.

> Can you attach an example that shows the issue?  (Must be openly licensed, ideally in test_regress format.)

```verilog
module test;
  localparam int unsigned SPI_AHB_INDEX = 0;
  localparam int unsigned I2C_AHB_INDEX = 1;

  localparam bit AHB_USE_IO_BASE[2] = '{
    SPI_AHB_INDEX: 1'b0,
    I2C_AHB_INDEX: 1'b0};

  localparam logic [31:0] AHB_ADDR[2] = '{
    SPI_AHB_INDEX: 32'h8001000,
    I2C_AHB_INDEX: 32'h8002000};

endmodule
```

> What 'verilator' command line do we use to run your example?

```
$ verilator --lint-only test.sv
%Error: test.sv:6:5: Assignment pattern key not supported/understood: TEXT
                   : ... note: In instance 'test'
    6 |     SPI_AHB_INDEX: 1'b0,
      |     ^~~~~~~~~~~~~
%Error: test.sv:7:5: Assignment pattern key not supported/understood: TEXT
                   : ... note: In instance 'test'
    7 |     I2C_AHB_INDEX: 1'b0};
      |     ^~~~~~~~~~~~~
%Error: test.sv:10:5: Assignment pattern key not supported/understood: TEXT
                    : ... note: In instance 'test'
   10 |     SPI_AHB_INDEX: 32'h8001000,
      |     ^~~~~~~~~~~~~
%Error: test.sv:11:5: Assignment pattern key not supported/understood: TEXT
                    : ... note: In instance 'test'
   11 |     I2C_AHB_INDEX: 32'h8002000};
      |     ^~~~~~~~~~~~~
%Error: Exiting due to 4 error(s)
```

> What 'verilator --version' are you using?  Did you try it with the git master version?

```
$ verilator --version
Verilator 5.031 devel rev v5.030-23-g2e1128b41
```

> What OS and distribution are you using?

Ubuntu 24.04 (via wsl)

> May we assist you in trying to fix this in Verilator yourself?

Yes, I see the error is being generated in V3Width:patVectorMap
At this stage the localparams appear as a Text object, And this function is only handling Const objects. 
I haven't dug much deeper into the code yet.
